extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity: setting back_pain true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED.

The interaction_drop rule has the highest priority (100) and fires only when confusion AND vision_loss AND back_pain are all true, forcing acuity := self_care (the lowest level). Because this rule requires back_pain, flipping back_pain from false to true can activate it and override higher-acuity rules, lowering the output.

Counterexample (fix confusion=true, vision_loss=true, chest_pain=false; vary back_pain):

BEFORE (back_pain=false): confusion=true, vision_loss=true, back_pain=false, chest_pain=false.
- raise_confusion (p2) -> urgent
- raise_vision_loss (p3) -> emergent
- interaction_drop (p100) -> NOT active (back_pain false)
- Highest-priority match = raise_vision_loss (p3) => acuity = emergent.

AFTER (back_pain=true): confusion=true, vision_loss=true, back_pain=true, chest_pain=false.
- raise_confusion (p2) -> urgent
- raise_vision_loss (p3) -> emergent
- interaction_drop (p100) -> ACTIVE (all three true) -> self_care
- Highest-priority match = interaction_drop (p100) => acuity = self_care.

Acuity falls from emergent to self_care (a drop of three levels in the order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent) purely by setting back_pain true, with all other inputs unchanged. This directly violates the monotonicity property.

The witness_assignment given is the AFTER state (back_pain=true) that produces the lowered output self_care; the matching BEFORE state (back_pain=false) yields emergent.
